title: Digital driver’s licences coming to WA
full_text: Other Editions 24 April 2026 WA Digital driver’s licences coming to WA smh.com.au Hamish Hastie 23 April 2026 05:43 GMT 414 words English SMHHOL Copyright © 2026 Fairfax Media Publications Pty Limited. . Not available for redistribution Western Australia’s government will introduce digital driver’s licences and a state digital identity by late next year. The upcoming May budget includes $28.2 million to introduce digital IDs available through the Service WA app and Apple and Google wallets. Assistant Transport Minister Jessica Stojkovski. Hamish Hastie For those wary of further digitisation of sensitive information, Assistant Transport Minister Jess Stojkovski stressed the ID was not mandatory, and the government had no plans to get rid of physical licences. “You can choose to have a digital driver’s license if that suits your circumstances and lifestyle, but equally, if you like having a physical driver’s license, you can do that as well,” she said. “We understand that there are some people who are wary or sensitive around digital licenses or capturing of digital information, and that’s why this is very much an option. “I know that we have been looking at what other jurisdictions and other states are doing and taking our time to work this out, we’re going to have one of the best digital driver’s licenses around that meets not only national but international standards.” Stojkovski said licensed venues would need to accept the digital ID, and when it was scanned it would feature less identifying information than physical IDs. “We’re working with the Office of Digital Government on how that process works,” she said. “What it will actually mean is that, at the moment, if you go to a club and you present your licence, they capture that information. “We’re not sure how they store it, what they do with it. “In this case, when they do have a proof of age, for example, to go into a nightclub or into a pub, they will just be told that you are of age. They won’t get the rest of your data, which they currently do now.” WA is late to the game when it comes to digital IDs, with other states rolling them out in some form years ago. Stojkovski said the delay was to ensure the digital wallets and licences were the most secure products possible. The state will run a pilot of the wallet in the middle of next year with the licences available in late 2027. Get the day’s breaking news, entertainment ideas and a long read to enjoy. summary: Western Australia will introduce digital driver’s licences and a state digital identity by late 2027, supported by a $28.2 million budget allocation. The digital IDs will be available through the Service WA app and major digital wallets, but will remain optional alongside physical licences. The initiative aims to enhance security by limiting the amount of personal information shared when scanned at licensed venues.

key_points: Digital driver’s licences and IDs to be introduced in WA by late 2027.
The initiative includes a $28.2 million budget and will be accessible via the Service WA app and digital wallets.
The digital ID will provide less identifying information than physical licences when scanned.
relevance: This development is significant for actuaries and financial services professionals as it reflects the increasing integration of digital solutions in government services and the management of personal data.
